channel.title: France 24 - International breaking news, top stories and headlines
channel.description: Breaking news and world news from France 24 on Business, Sports, Culture. Video news. News from the US, Europe, Asia Pacific, Africa, Middle East, America

item.title: UK: Outrage grows after police handcuff dying student
item.description: British police faced national backlash over the inflammatory case of an 18-year-old student who was handcuffed as he lay dying from stab wounds after his killer falsely alleged a racist attack. The killing of Henry Nowak happened in December, but the story got renewed attention after the killer was sentenced to life in prison on Monday, and video was released that showed police not believing Nowak when he said that he had been stabbed.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Benin's creators revive national history through video games
item.description: In Benin, creators are turning to digital games to help young people learn about their country's history and explore their cultural heritage in a new way - whether through video games or augmented reality.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kenyans protest femicide scourge and denounce government inaction
item.description: Hundreds of protesters marched through Kenya's capital on June 1 against the high number of killings of women and children. At least 69 women have been killed since the beginning of the year, with official data also recording over 10,000 missing children over the past sixteen months.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kenyan opposition to Ebola quarantine centre for US citizens grows
item.description: In Kenya, the High Court has extended the suspension order against the implementation of a controversial agreement between Kenya and the United States that includes plans for an Ebola quarantine facility for Americans suspected of being infected with the virus. This comes amid protests over said centre, during which two people have died. The court has also prohibited the transfer of patients from third countries to Kenya. FRANCE 24's Bastien Renouil tells us more from Nairobi.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Strikes on Russian strategic sites show Ukraine 'ramping up long-range strikes'
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit energy and military sites in Saint Petersburg as officials gathered for a flagship economic forum in the city. The strikes come a day after a barrage of Russian missiles and drones killed 23 people across Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani looks at why those strikes are so significant.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Pope names first lay woman as head of a Vatican department
item.description: For the first time in Vatican history, a lay woman will head the Vatican’s Dicastery for Communication. Maria Montserrat Alvarado, CEO of a Catholic television network, will be in charge of the vast apparatus surrounding the Pope. Television, radio, broadcasting... A clear commitment to continuing Pope Francis’ legacy of modernizing the Catholic Church and giving women a greater role.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: 'El Niño turbocharging climate change', warns Gareth Redmond-King
item.description: Oliver Farry is pleased to welcome Gareth Redmond-King, Head of the International Programme at the Energy & Climate Intelligence Unit. As the World Meteorological Organization warns of a powerful El Niño event and its potentially far-reaching consequences, Redmond-King warns that El Niño should not be viewed as an isolated threat, but rather as an accelerant acting upon an already destabilised climate system. El Niño is "pouring fuel on that particular fire" of climate change. He describes the phenomenon as "turbocharging climate change" by adding heat to a warming planet.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Israel strikes hospital in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel continues to hit targets in Lebanon despite a partial US-brokered ceasefire in place. Lebanese and Israeli diplomats are in Washington for the fourth round of direct talks, but those negotiations have been strongly opposed by Hezbollah. Pressure also mounting within Netanyahu's own government to continue the war in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Chinese-linked tech company uses AI to predict who might become a dissident
item.description: PRESS REVIEW – Wednesday, June 3, 2026: The British front pages discuss the murder of 18-year-old student Henry Nowak after the release of shocking police bodycam footage. Next, the Trump administration's appointment of the new director of national intelligence is getting a lot of coverage. Also, The Times reveals that a Chinese state-linked tech company is using AI to predict who might become a dissident. Finally, football and fashion mix on the way to the World Cup.
item.dc:creator: Emily BOYLE

item.title: 'I can't breathe': protesters clash with police over handcuffed dying student
item.description: Protests erupted in the UK after a video emerged of a dying student being handcuffed by police. Eighteen-year-old Henry Nowak was heard repeatedly telling officers: "I can't breathe" after he was stabbed by a Sikh man who falsely accused him of racial abuse. The far right have seized on the case, saying white British people are treated as second class citizens by the police. This is despite a UK government report in 2024 which found that black people were 2.2 times as likely to be arrested as white people. The UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer said the bodycam footage was "harrowing" and there would be an investigation into police conduct.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: As California votes in the primary elections, who are the candidates?
item.description: US primaries are taking place in six states. The voting will shape the landscape of the midterms in November, as it decides who is going to be on the ballot. Key races to look out for include California, where whoever wins the race for Los Angeles mayor will lead the city during the Olympic Games.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: New round of Israel-Lebanon talks opens after Israeli strikes
item.description: The ambassadors of Israel and Lebanon began a new round of direct talks in Washington on after US President Donald Trump said he had received commitments to de-escalation from both sides. The fourth meeting between representatives of the two countries, which do not have diplomatic relations, is taking place at the State Department and is scheduled to last two days. It comes as Israeli drone strikes on southern Lebanon on June 2 killed eight people, including two children and their father, and as Iran-backed Hezbollah continued launching drones at Israeli troops.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Kuwait airport hit by Iranian drones as US and Iran trade fire
item.description: The fragile ceasefire in the US Iran war was tested overnight as Tehran launched a series of drone and missile attacks on Kuwait, hitting and severely damaging its international airport. Kuwaiti authorities have insisted that missiles are being intercepted while the US has launched what it calls self-defensive strikes on the Iranian military ground control station on Qeshm Island in the Strait of Hormuz in response. It comes as negotiations between Washington and Tehran appear to be stalling.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Frederiksen to remain Denmark's PM leading centre-left coalition government
item.description: Denmark's government has finally been unveiled after the country's general election back in March. Mette Frederiksen will return for a third term as prime minister, leading a centre-left coalition minority government. She is expected to maintain a firm stance against US President Donald Trump's ambitions for Greenland and to continue to support Ukraine. On a domestic front, she's vowed improve the cost of living.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: Californian elections test for divided Democrats on pivotal primary election day
item.description: US primaries are taking place in six states. The voting will shape the landscape of the midterms in November, as it decides who is going to be on the ballot. Key races to look out for include California, where candidates will be looking to replace term-limited Gavin Newsom. FRANCE 24's Wassim Cornet, in Los Angeles, and Philip Turle tell us more.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: NATO chief on unannounced visit to Kyiv after deadly Russian attacks
item.description: NATO chief Mark Rutte arrived in Kyiv on June 3 for an unannounced trip after a series of large-scale fatal Russian attacks on the Ukrainian capital. It also comes as Ukrainian drones hit Saint Petersburg just as the city's flagship economic forum opens. FRANCE 24's Emmanuelle Chaze has more from Kyiv.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘Chased like dogs’: Anti-migrant mobs in South Africa force foreigners to flee
item.description: Hundreds of foreign nationals have been forced to flee their homes in South Africa after violent anti-migrant mobs began chasing them down. Weeks of violent protests have led to the deaths of at least five nationals of Mozambique, that country's government said, adding that roughly 300 had fled back over its border Saturday. Ghana and Nigeria have organised emergency repatriation flights.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: France's recognition of its role in 1994 Rwanda genocide marks historic turning point
item.description: François Picard is pleased to welcome Juliette Bour, historian and author of a forthcoming book in French entitled "Like Men: Women Politicians Complicit in the Rwandan Genocide". More than three decades after the 1994 genocide against the Tutsi in Rwanda, France continues to confront difficult questions about its political, military and diplomatic role during one of the most horrific atrocities of the 20th century. The inauguration of a memorial and monument in Paris, attended by President Emmanuel Macron, marks another step in a long and contentious process of historical reckoning.
item.dc:creator: François PICARD

item.title: Live: Russian warship at Baltic Fleet base struck overnight, Ukraine says
item.description: The Ukrainian military said Wednesday it struck Russia’s Boikiy warship at its Baltic Fleet base overnight, causing a massive fire aboard the ship. Russia said it would continue to strike Ukraine "systematically" after Ukrainian drones hit an oil terminal and naval base in Saint Petersburg. The attack comes as the city stands host for an economic conference known as the “Russian Davos” and which President Vladimir Putin is set to attend. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: French Open: Zverev reaches semis as Grand Slam dream lives on
item.description: Alexander Zverev defeated Rafael Jodar (7-6, 6-1, 6-3) in the quarter-finals at Roland Garros. Having now reached the semi-finals, he can still hope to win his first Grand Slam title.
item.dc:creator: L'Equipe TV

item.title: Japan keeps traditional samurai horse festival alive by adapting to climate change
item.description: For more than a millennium, Japan has celebrated its samurai heritage with armoured horseriders charging through the countryside during its annual Soma Nomaoi festival. Up until two years ago, the tradition was threatened by soaring summer temperatures brought on by climate change. Organisers have decided to adapt, relieving riders and horses alike by switching the event to the spring.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: Colombia's presidential frontrunner vows closer US ties after Trump endorsement
item.description: Colombia's hard-right presidential frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella pledged closer ties with the United States on Tuesday after receiving US President Donald Trump's endorsement, strengthening his position ahead of a June 21 run-off election against leftist senator Ivan Cepeda.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24

item.title: US military says it intercepted Iranian missile and drone attacks in Gulf
item.dc:creator: FRANCE24

item.title: ‘We love Wemby’: San Antonio embraces French star ahead of NBA Finals
item.description: Since Frenchman Victor “Wemby” Wembanyama came to San Antonio, locals say the Spurs have opened a new chapter and are confident the young basketballer will help the team secure a sixth title in the upcoming NBA final. "It's a great team. At the very top is Wemby; when he's on the court, everything just works better. Everyone contributes," one of his many local supporters said.
item.dc:creator: FRANCE 24
